generatedSentence: Elias, a once virtuous architect nearing retirement, meticulously reviewed the blueprints for the new city sector. His life felt like a default setting, riddled with systemic defects he couldn't fix, constantly under the gaze of corporate watchers. He dreaded the enquiry that would inevitably expose the dirty secrets, feeling a tightening in his neck, a neurone misfiring with a constant jinx of unease. He attempted a jocose demeanor during his late-night gaming sessions, often ordering fajitas as a small comfort. His professional license offered little lenitive against the whispers of backstabbing within his firm. The project, supposedly tiptop, was suspected of involving massive payoffs designed to harass smaller competitors. A recent medical checkup revealed nothing, but he knew the monitors of the firm were always on. This edition of city planning reflected a truly dystopian vision, its extreme density controlled by powerful signal jammers. The unexpected discovery sent Elias reeling, his mind a madhouse of frantic thoughts. He'd stumbled upon schematics for a secret takeoff platform, designed to launch an unknown craft. A faint twinkle in the dark corners of the blueprints hinted at something more. He felt a sudden thirsty desperation, realizing the danger. The aftershocks of this revelation would be immense. He suspected an urbane figure, recently hired as a consultant, was behind it. The constant thrum of a distant jackhammer seemed to mock his efforts. This clandestine operation was the crowning achievement of an occult cabal within the corporation, a truly titanic endeavor. They planned a massive data rollover, hoping to hide their tracks. He remembered a colleague, an enthusiast of hockey and an expert on local wildlife, including the rare seagull that nested nearby, who had vanished. The blueprints spoke of a structure that would pierce space, requiring the muscle of disenfranchised dockworkers to build. It depicted strange, almost jungle-like biomes, with structures resembling a distant giraffe or a swirling whirlpool, and a faint, almost ghostly aura surrounding them. Elias knew he had to counter their plans. He, an unlikely finalist in this deadly game, recalled ancient verses about overcoming dark forces. He found himself on a dilapidated veranda, peering through a broken window. His thoughts drifted to his estranged sweetheart, a woman he’d loved, remembering a fleeting moment of peace after his vasectomy years ago. He felt luckiest to have his mind still sharp, a genuinely liberal outlook in a world of rigid control. Suddenly, a dog’s sharp bark echoed, startling him. He clutched his knapsack, a heavy bucket of water splashing as he moved. He'd seen reports of large buffalo herds being displaced by the project. He felt unstuck from his previous apathy, a new purpose igniting. He had to cross the crumbling rampart of the old city wall. A cold, gelid wind carried faint whiffs of cheap whiskey and ginger from a nearby pub. He prepared for flight, his optic nerve tingling with adrenaline. A sudden visitor appeared, a hulking figure, their presence a silent, verbal threat, signaling his immediate mortal danger. He felt a sharp pain in his pelvis as he stumbled. A strange liquid spilled from the broken device, dissolving the data, a silent victory. He wasn’t a teacher, but his actions had taught a lesson. The ensuing affrays shook the city, but Elias, feeling like an anchor in the storm, was safe. The once unfriendly corporate entity was now a mere knockoff of its former power. There would be no public reward, but the sense of calm was enough. The rich humus of the city’s reclaimed green spaces symbolized renewal. He imagined his former colleagues, surely envious of his newfound peace. He spent his days cultivating vegetables in a small garden, occasionally enjoying salsa he made himself. He gifted handcrafted dreamcatchers to friends, reflecting on the ninety percent of his life spent in corporate servitude. Though his fingers felt numb from the cold, his spirit began to jubilate. He had used a small jimmy to open the access panel, and then he was whisked away to safety. The corporate glacier of greed had finally begun to melt, its destructive flame extinguished. He occasionally crafted intricate origami figures, listening to distant violins, a testament to the resilient spirit of mankind. He felt limber, ready for new beginnings, perhaps even a casual barbeque with old friends.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, modern noir graphic novel style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A grizzled, middle-aged man with a short, graying beard and tired eyes, wearing a rumpled collared shirt and slacks. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The man at his desk, staring intensely at a holographic blueprint display, close-up on his face showing strain. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The man secretly observing a massive, unusual structure, partially hidden by fog, resembling a futuristic launchpad with strange, organic-like features, from a distance, looking determined, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The man scrambling over crumbling industrial ramparts, clutching a knapsack, a large dog barking in the foreground, dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The man, older and serene, tending a small urban garden with vibrant vegetables, a faint city skyline in the background, medium shot focusing on his peaceful expression. All human characters are visibly adult with mature features, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting.

definitions.definition: Full of virtue, having excellent moral character.
definitions.example: Successful communities need strong, selfless leaders and a virtuous people.

definitions.definition: A house where insane persons are confined; an insane asylum.
(by extension) A chaotic place.
A type of paper-based reproduction process producing white-on-blue images, used primarily for technical and architecture's drawings, now largely replaced by other technologies.
A print produced with this process.
(by extension) A detailed technical drawing (now often in some electronically storable and transmissible form).
(by extension) Any detailed plan, whether literal or figurative.
To make a blueprint for.
definitions.example: The architect blueprinted the renovation plan once the client had signed off.

definitions.definition: To make a detailed operational plan for.
definitions.example: They blueprinted every aspect of the first phase of the operation.

definitions.definition: The rising or ascent of an aircraft or rocket into flight.
definitions.example: The flight was smooth, but the takeoff was a little rough.

definitions.definition: A parody or lampoon of someone or something.
A quantification, especially of building materials.
definitions.example: I'll give you an estimate after I do the quantity takeoffs for the trusses and structural steel.

definitions.definition: The removal of sheets from the press.
The spot from which one takes off; specifically, the place from which a jumper rises in leaping.
A sparkle or glimmer of light
A sparkle of delight in the eyes.
definitions.example: He was a rotund, jolly man with a twinkle in his eye.

definitions.definition: A flitting movement
A brief moment; a twinkling.
The female genitalia.
definitions.example: The popular Swedish cartoon song about genitals was translated as "Willie and Twinkle".

definitions.definition: (of a source of light) to shine with a flickering light; to glimmer
definitions.example: We could see the lights of the village twinkling in the distance.

definitions.definition: (chiefly of eyes) to be bright with delight
definitions.example: His shrewd little eyes twinkled roguishly.

definitions.definition: To bat, blink or wink the eyes
To flit to and fro
Not expected, anticipated or foreseen.
The condition of failing to meet an obligation.
definitions.example: He failed to make payments on time and is now in default.

definitions.definition: The original software programming settings as set by the factory
A loss incurred by failing to compete.
definitions.example: The team's three losses include one default.

definitions.definition: A selection made in the absence of an alternative.
definitions.example: The man became the leader of the group as a default.

definitions.definition: A value used when none has been given; a tentative value or standard that is presumed.
definitions.example: If you don't specify a number of items, the default is 1.

definitions.definition: The failure of a defendant to appear and answer a summons and complaint.
A failing or failure; omission of that which ought to be done; neglect to do what duty or law requires.
definitions.example: This evil has happened through the governor's default.

definitions.definition: Lack; absence.
Fault; offence; wrong act.
To fail to meet an obligation.
definitions.example: If you do not make your payments, you will default on your loan.

definitions.definition: To lose a competition by failing to compete.
definitions.example: If you refuse to wear a proper uniform, you will not be allowed to compete and will default this match.

definitions.definition: To assume a value when none was given; to presume a tentative value or standard.
definitions.example: If you don't specify a number of items, it defaults to 1.

definitions.definition: To fail to appear and answer a summons and complaint.
A fault or malfunction.
definitions.example: a defect in the ear or eye; a defect in timber or iron; a defect of memory or judgment

definitions.definition: The quantity or amount by which anything falls short.
A part by which a figure or quantity is wanting or deficient.
To abandon or turn against; to cease or change one's loyalty, especially from a military organisation or political party.
To desert one's army, to flee from combat.
To join the enemy army.
To flee one's country and seek asylum.
Someone who watches or observes.
Someone who keeps vigil.
A guard.

Matter in a state intermediate between liquid and plasma that can be contained only if it is fully surrounded by a solid (or in a bubble of liquid) (or held together by gravitational pull); it can condense into a liquid, or can (rarely) become a solid directly.
definitions.example: A lot of gas had escaped from the cylinder.

definitions.definition: A chemical element or compound in such a state.
definitions.example: The atmosphere is made up of a number of different gases.

definitions.definition: A flammable gaseous hydrocarbon or hydrocarbon mixture (typically predominantly methane) used as a fuel, e.g. for cooking, heating, electricity generation or as a fuel in internal combustion engines in vehicles.
definitions.example: Gas-fired power stations have largely replaced coal-burning ones.

definitions.definition: A hob on a gas cooker.
definitions.example: She turned the gas on, put the potatoes on, then lit the oven.

definitions.definition: Methane or other waste gases trapped in one's belly as a result of the digestive process.
definitions.example: My tummy hurts so bad, I have gas.

definitions.definition: A humorous or entertaining event or person.
definitions.example: He is such a gas!

definitions.definition: Frothy talk; chatter.
A fastball.
definitions.example: The closer threw him nothing but gas.

definitions.definition: Arterial or venous blood gas.
Marijuana, typically of high quality.
To kill with poisonous gas.
To talk in a boastful or vapid way; chatter.
To impose upon by talking boastfully.
To emit gas.
definitions.example: The battery cell was gassing.

definitions.definition: To impregnate with gas.
definitions.example: to gas lime with chlorine in the manufacture of bleaching powder

definitions.definition: To singe, as in a gas flame, so as to remove loose fibers.
definitions.example: to gas thread

definitions.definition: A cut of quick-cured pork leg.
To cure bacon by salting.
To discipline (one's body, appetites etc.) by suppressing desires; to practise abstinence on.
definitions.example: Some people seek sainthood by mortifying the body.

definitions.definition: (usually used passively) To embarrass, to humiliate. To injure one's dignity.
definitions.example: I was so mortified I could have died right there; instead I fainted, but I swore I'd never let that happen to me again.

definitions.definition: To kill.
To reduce the potency of; to nullify; to deaden, neutralize.
To kill off (living tissue etc.); to make necrotic.
To affect with vexation, chagrin, or humiliation; to humble; to depress.
To grant in mortmain.
To lose vitality.
To gangrene.
To be subdued.
Needing to drink.
definitions.example: After all that work I am really thirsty.

definitions.definition: Causing thirst; giving one a need to drink (informal).
Craving something.
definitions.example: After the president left office, the nation was thirsty for change.

definitions.definition: Desiring sex.
definitions.example: This is the third time that girl brought up her crush. She must really be thirsty for her.

definitions.definition: An earthquake that follows in the same vicinity as another, usually larger, earthquake (the "mainshock").
By extension, any result or consequence following a major event.
Emotional and physical distress following a traumatic event.
(of a man) Courteous, polite, refined, and suave.
definitions.example: 1949: George Orwell, Nineteen Eighty-Four, p12

definitions.definition: To obtain the services of in return for fixed payment.
definitions.example: We hired a car for two weeks because ours had broken down.

definitions.definition: To employ; to obtain the services of (a person) in exchange for remuneration; to give someone a job.
definitions.example: The company had problems when it tried to hire more skilled workers.

definitions.definition: To exchange the services of for remuneration.
definitions.example: They hired themselves out as day laborers.  They hired out their basement for Inauguration week.

definitions.definition: To accomplish by paying for services.
definitions.example: After waiting two years for her husband to finish the tiling, she decided to hire it done.

definitions.definition: To accept employment.
definitions.example: They hired out as day laborers.

definitions.definition: A question.
Search for truth, information or knowledge.
definitions.example: scientific enquiry

definitions.definition: To make (something) dirty.
To stain or tarnish (somebody) with dishonor.
To debase by distorting the real nature of (something).
To become soiled.
Unclean; covered with or containing unpleasant substances such as dirt or grime.
definitions.example: Despite a walk in the rain, my shoes weren't too dirty.

definitions.definition: That makes one unclean; corrupting, infecting.
definitions.example: Don't put that in your mouth, dear. It's dirty.

definitions.definition: Morally unclean; obscene or indecent, especially sexually.
definitions.example: At the reception, Uncle Nick got drunk and told dirty jokes to the bridesmaids.

definitions.definition: Dishonourable; violating accepted standards or rules.
definitions.example: He might have scored, but it was a dirty trick that won him the penalty.

definitions.definition: Corrupt, illegal, or improper.
definitions.example: I won't accept your dirty money!

definitions.definition: Out of tune.
definitions.example: You need to tune that guitar: the G string sounds dirty.

definitions.definition: Of color, discolored by impurities.
definitions.example: The old flag was a dirty white.

definitions.definition: Containing data needing to be written back to memory or disk.
definitions.example: Occasionally it reads the sector into a dirty buffer, which means it needs to sync the dirty buffer first.

definitions.definition: Carrying illegal drugs among one's possessions or inside of one's bloodstream.
definitions.example: None of y'all get into my car if you're dirty.

definitions.definition: Used as an intensifier, especially in conjunction with "great".
definitions.example: He lives in a dirty great mansion.

definitions.definition: Sleety; gusty; stormy.
definitions.example: dirty weather

definitions.definition: Of an alcoholic beverage, especially a cocktail or mixed drink: served with the juice of olives.
definitions.example: dirty martini

definitions.definition: Of food, indulgent in an unhealthy way.
definitions.example: The waiter served dirty burgers to the customers.

definitions.definition: In a dirty manner.
definitions.example: to play dirty

definitions.definition: The part of the body connecting the head and the trunk found in humans and some animals.
definitions.example: Giraffes have long necks.

definitions.definition: The corresponding part in some other anatomical contexts.
The part of a shirt, dress etc., which fits a person's neck.
The tapered part of a bottle toward the opening.
The slender tubelike extension atop an archegonium, through which the sperm swim to reach the egg.
The extension of any stringed instrument on which a fingerboard is mounted
A long narrow tract of land projecting from the main body, or a narrow tract connecting two larger tracts.
A reduction in size near the end of an object, formed by a groove around it.
definitions.example: a neck forming the journal of a shaft

definitions.definition: The constriction between the root and crown of a tooth.
The gorgerin of a capital.
The small part of a gun between the chase and the swell of the muzzle.
A person's life.
definitions.example: to risk one's neck; to save someone's neck

definitions.definition: A falsehood; a lie.
Shapeshifting water spirits in Germanic mythology and folklore
To hang by the neck; strangle; kill, eliminate
definitions.example: Go neck yourself.

definitions.definition: To make love; to intently kiss or cuddle; to canoodle.
definitions.example: Alan and Betty were necking in the back of a car when Betty's dad caught them.

definitions.definition: To drink rapidly.
To decrease in diameter.
A cell of the nervous system, which conducts nerve impulses; consisting of an axon and several dendrites. Neurons are connected by synapses.
A nervure of an insect's wing.
An artificial neuron (mathematical function serving as an essential unit of an artificial neural network)
A hex; an evil spell.
A person or thing supposed to bring bad luck.
To cast a spell on.
To bring bad luck to.
To cause something to happen by mentioning it, usually sarcastically.
Used after the same response is said by two people simultaneously.
Given to jesting; habitually jolly
Playful; characterized by joking
A portable percussive power tool that combines a hammer and chisel used to drill or break hard matter, for instance rock or concrete.
To use a jackhammer.
To break (something) using a jackhammer.
To form (something) using a jackhammer.
To move like a jackhammer.
To move (something) like a jackhammer.
To strike (something) repeatedly with force, to pound.
To gamble.
To play card games, board games, or video games.
To exploit loopholes in a system or bureaucracy in a way which defeats or nullifies the spirit of the rules in effect, usually to obtain a result which otherwise would be unobtainable.
definitions.example: We'll bury them in paperwork, and game the system.

definitions.definition: (of males) To perform premeditated seduction strategy.
The playing of a game or games, including but not limited to video games and games of chance.
Careful, strategic use of rules to achieve one's purposes, as one would use the rules of a game.
To place a crown on the head of.
To formally declare (someone) a king, queen, emperor, etc.
To bestow something upon as a mark of honour, dignity, or recompense; to adorn; to dignify.
To form the topmost or finishing part of; to complete; to consummate; to perfect.
To declare (someone) a winner.
Of a baby, during the birthing process; for the surface of the baby's head to appear in the vaginal opening.
definitions.example: The mother was in the second stage of labor and the fetus had just crowned, prompting a round of encouragement from the midwives.

definitions.definition: To cause to round upward; to make anything higher at the middle than at the edges, such as the face of a machine pulley.
To hit on the head.
To shoot an opponent in the back of the head with a shotgun in a first-person shooter video game.
In checkers, to stack two checkers to indicate that the piece has become a king.
definitions.example: “Crown me!” I said, as I moved my checker to the back row.

definitions.definition: To widen the opening of the barrel.
To effect a lodgment upon, as upon the crest of the glacis, or the summit of the breach.
To lay the ends of the strands of (a knot) over and under each other.
Supreme; of a surpassing quality or quantity.
definitions.example: a crowning achievement

definitions.definition: A Tex-Mex dish of strips of spicy marinated meat and/or vegetables in a soft flour tortilla, often served with salad or a savoury filling.
(usually with "the") Supernatural affairs.
To cover or hide from view.
definitions.example: The Earth occults the Moon during a lunar eclipse.

definitions.definition: To dissimulate, conceal, or obfuscate.
Secret; hidden from general knowledge; undetected.
definitions.example: occult blood loss;  occult cancer

definitions.definition: Related to the occult; pertaining to mysticism, magic, or astrology.
Esoteric.
A legal document giving official permission to do something; a permit.
The legal terms under which a person is allowed to use a product, especially software.
Freedom to deviate deliberately from normally applicable rules or practices (especially in behaviour or speech).
Excessive freedom; lack of due restraint.
Short for driver's license.
definitions.example: In order to enter the building, I need to show my license.

definitions.definition: To give a formal (usually written) authorization.
definitions.example: It was decided to license Wikipedia under the GFDL.

definitions.definition: Authorize officially.
definitions.example: I am licensed to practice law in this state.

definitions.definition: An analgesic or other source of relief from pain
A laxative.
Analgesic, able to reduce pain or suffering.
Laxative; easing the bowels.
(of a person) Mild; gentle.

To attack someone (especially verbally) unfairly in a deceitful, underhand, or treacherous manner, especially when they're not present in the place or situation that it happens. (as if stabbing them in the back). See backbite.
The act of one who backstabs.
Having great size, or great strength, force or power.
The very topmost point.
The highest or utmost degree; the best of anything.
Excellently; in a first-rate manner.
A branching and bony structure on the head of deer, moose and elk, normally in pairs. They are grown and shed each year. (Compare with horn, which is generally not shed.)
definitions.example: While hiking in the woods, I found an antler from a deer.

definitions.definition: The process of incrementing, especially back to an initial value.
A road traffic accident in which a vehicle overturns.
A graphic element that changes its appearance when the cursor moves over it.
A keyboard feature where each key is scanned independently, so that multiple simultaneous keypresses always register correctly.
The reinvestment of funds in a new issue of the same or similar investment.
A fee paid by a borrower in order to defer full repayment of a loan.
In the National or European lottery, the situation in which a jackpot that has not been won is carried over to the next week.
A target on the pinball table that is activated when the ball rolls over it.
A payment.
A reward.
A bribe.
